Income_lt_25k_Age_lt_30.description: Percentage of respondents with income < $25,000 and age < 30 years. Part of the two-way table of age-income classes.

Income_25k_50k_Age_lt_30.description: Percentage of respondents with income $25,000–$50,000 and age < 30 years. Part of the two-way table of age-income classes.

Income_gt_50k_Age_lt_30.description: Percentage of respondents with income > $50,000 and age < 30 years. Part of the two-way table; falls in event A (third column).

Income_lt_25k_Age_30_50.description: Percentage of respondents with income < $25,000 and age 30–50 years. Part of the two-way table; falls in event B (last two rows).

Income_25k_50k_Age_30_50.description: Percentage of respondents with income $25,000–$50,000 and age 30–50 years. Part of the two-way table; falls in event B (last two rows).

Income_gt_50k_Age_30_50.description: Percentage of respondents with income > $50,000 and age 30–50 years. Part of the two-way table; falls in both events A and B.

Income_lt_25k_Age_gt_50.description: Percentage of respondents with income < $25,000 and age > 50 years. Part of the two-way table; falls in event B (last two rows).

Income_25k_50k_Age_gt_50.description: Percentage of respondents with income $25,000–$50,000 and age > 50 years. Part of the two-way table; falls in event B (last two rows).

Income_gt_50k_Age_gt_50.description: Percentage of respondents with income > $50,000 and age > 50 years. Part of the two-way table; falls in both events A and B.
